message:
Last summer we lost power for about six hours, and I learned something humbling: my kids were WAY calmer about it than I was. I'm standing there mentally cataloging all the food about to spoil, and they're like "Mom, can we build a fort with the couch cushions?" Meanwhile, my phone was at 2%, and I'm stress-scrolling instead of, you know, actually doing something useful. My oldest finally asked, "Don't you have that battery thing?" — yes. Yes, I did. In a junk drawer. Unused.

Turns out the real blackout prep isn't about panicking into action last-minute. It's about having a few simple things ready *before* you need them, so when the lights go out, you can actually... relax a little. Help your kid build that fort instead of white-knuckling your phone.

Has a power outage ever caught you more unprepared than you'd like to admit? 😅
